subject: FW: New Year
body:
From: David Grosof [mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu] 
Sent: Wednesday, January 02, 2008 5:21 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Cc: Darren K Indyke, Esq.
Subject: New Year

 

Hi, Lesley,
 Here's a message for Jeffrey.
 Thanks for passing it on.
 
Jeffrey,
 I am hoping you have a very happy and healthy New Year. In struggles
ahead, I hope you win. Let me know what I can do to help.

 It's been difficult for me to understand what to do and now to expect
in supporting your science foundation work: I've barely heard from you
in ages, various questions and requests were posed to you but not
answered or even deferred, and I was paid in '06 for our consulting
agreement's six months but not at all since, which makes me feel rather
weird. I'd like to get further insight into your interests to provide
guidance about postal mail to be sent you if and when that's needed as
well as to support your science philanthropy. I want to support you and
not bother you during a busy and stressful time, but  I do feel I should
pointedly wish us a more communicative New Year.
When I spoke in fall 2006 with several scientists you'd supported about
what and how you'd enabled them to do, I was really proud to be
associated with you.

Cheers,
David
body_html:
From: David Grosof [mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, January 02, 2008 5:21 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Cc: Darren K Indyke, Esq.
Subject: New Year

 

Hi, Lesley,
 Here's a message for Jeffrey.
 Thanks for passing it on.
 
Jeffrey,
 I am hoping you have a very happy and healthy New Year. In struggles ahead, I hope you win. Let me know what I can do to help.

 It's been difficult for me to understand what to do and now to expect in supporting your science foundation work: I've barely heard from you in ages, various questions and requests were posed to you but not answered or even deferred, and I was paid in '06 for our consulting agreement's six months but not at all since, which makes me feel rather weird. I'd like to get further insight into your interests to provide guidance about postal mail to be sent you if and when that's needed as well as to support your science philanthropy. I want to support you and not bother you during a busy and stressful time, but  I do feel I should pointedly wish us a more communicative New Year.
When I spoke in fall 2006 with several scientists you'd supported about what and how you'd enabled them to do, I was really proud to be associated with you.

Cheers,
David
